description: E2 hypothesis was triple-attribution (labor + rebellion + stewardship) on the U6 cohort distributed across its members. Audit of the node body found canonical citations for LABOR (cosmic-class divine entities deploy cross-species mortal-warrior-labor in a god-wager-feud, per body's existing 'functional ideology' section) and for STEWARDSHIP (Vados-as-trainer-attendant per Dragon Ball Wiki's Vados page + Vegeta-mentors-Cabba canonical knowledge-transmission beat per Dragon Ball Wiki's Cabba page — added to body as new 'Stewardship-register internal to the cohort' bullet citing the destroyer-god-attendant-dyad node as the canonical-stewardship precedent register). REBELLION refused with receipts: the hypothesis-text 'Frost = corrupted-rebellion' is Z-canon antagonist-villainy not refusal-as-political-praxis, and the cohort is canonically compelled-into-tournament-deployment rather than refusing-the-apparatus. Verdict: dual-hub compression-point, NOT triple. related_hubs: [labor] → [labor, stewardship]. The first dual-attribution where the canonical-evidence is *distributed across cohort members* rather than carried by a single named individual.
citations: [Dragon Ball Wiki: Vados — angel attendant, Champa's combat instructor]
[Dragon Ball Wiki: Cabba — Vegeta-mentors-Cabba canonical training-beat and the cross-universe promise-to-protect register]
[Existing node body: 'cosmic-class destroyer-god deploys mortal-class warrior-labor' apparatus-of-extraction citation]
[[destroyer-god-attendant-dyad]] — structural precedent for the angel-attendant-stewardship register]
[[_triple-thesis]] — dual-hub compression-point cardinality discipline; refusal-with-receipts on the absent verb]
notes: Refusal-with-receipts on the rebellion-hypothesis is first-class output. The body's prior single-attribution `related_hubs: [labor]` was correct under the wave-67-commission citation set; the wave-73 audit surfaces the stewardship-register that was *latent in the existing prose* (Vados-as-trainer was always cited as 'Champa's combat instructor'; the [[destroyer-god-attendant-dyad]] node's later commission canonicalized that as stewardship-register). The dual-attribution is recovered, not invented. The corpus now has its first dual-attribution where the canonical-evidence is *distributed across cohort members* rather than carried by a single named individual — sibling pattern to single-character dual-attribution exemplars like [[bardock]] (labor + rebellion) and [[aether-foundation]] (labor + stewardship).
